description_long: This airplane features a sleek, futuristic design with a central cylindrical fuselage flanked by wide, sharply angled wings that give it a bat-like silhouette. The wings are equipped with large, circular engine housings and intricate mechanical details, suggesting advanced propulsion systems. The nose is pointed and metallic, with a multi-colored tip, while the rear section is compact and streamlined. The surface is predominantly dark with greenish metallic hues, accented by red and silver highlights, and displays a combination of smooth and slightly textured panels. Visible mechanical components, such as exposed gears and vents, add to its high-tech appearance. The overall form is compact yet wide, with a low profile and aggressive stance.
description: A futuristic, bat-winged airplane with a central cylindrical fuselage, wide angular wings, and prominent engine housings, finished in dark metallic and green hues with red accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the cylindrical fuselage, pointed nose, and one wing with visible engine housing and mechanical details.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide wingspan, central fuselage, and symmetrical engine housings on each wing.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with the same wing and engine details visible from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the compact tail section, dual engine housings, and the full angular spread of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept, angular wings and no visible winglets; wings feature anhedral angles and integrated engine housings.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the wings near the fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and low horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main units and a nose gear, partially visible.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single-seat based on size and design.
